name: Wrap-around Error
description: A value is incremented past the maximum value for its integer type and wraps around to a very small or negative value, causing unexpected behavior in size calculations, loop bounds, or memory allocations that can lead to buffer overflows or other memory corruption.
detection_pattern: TRANSFORM[arithmetic_increment] on an integer value in STORAGE exceeds the type's maximum (e.g., UINT_MAX, INT_MAX) and wraps to a small/negative value, which is then used in a CONTROL[size_check] or STORAGE[memory_alloc] without a CONTROL[overflow_check] that detects the wrap-around before the value is consumed
